@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2m0","title":"Onboarding polish: lesson 8 visibility + tailscale jq-optional + update warn","description":"Land small UX/robustness tweaks: ensure onboarding menus/progress account for lesson 8 (Keeping Updated); make tailscale status parsing work without jq; add warn-level output helper to update logger; and keep stack installers from forcing --yes in interactive mode.","acceptance_criteria":"- Onboard menus/progress include lesson 8 (total 9 lessons)\n- tailscale.sh parses BackendState without jq installed\n- update.sh supports warn log type\n- stack.sh only adds --yes flags when non-interactive","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:07:43.806195-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","close_reason":"Updated onboarding to include lesson 8; added jq-optional tailscale BackendState parsing; added warn log helper; and made stack installers respect interactive mode."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","title":"[ubu.2] Extend state.sh for upgrade tracking","description":"# Extend state.sh for Upgrade Tracking ## Purpose Add upgrade-specific state fields to track multi-reboot upgrade progress. ## State Schema Extensions Add new fields to state.json schema: ```json { "schema_version": 3, // Bump version for new fields // ... existing fields ... // NEW: Ubuntu upgrade tracking "ubuntu_upgrade": { "enabled": true, "started_at": "2025-12-21T10:00:00Z", "original_version": "24.04", "target_version": "25.10", "current_stage": "upgrading", "upgrade_path": ["24.04", "24.10", "25.04", "25.10"], "completed_upgrades": [ {"from": "24.04", "to": "24.10", "completed_at": "..."} ], "current_upgrade": {"from": "24.10", "to": "25.04", "started_at": "..."}, "needs_reboot": false, "resume_after_reboot": true, "last_error": null } } ``` ## Functions to Add ```bash # Initialize upgrade state state_upgrade_init() { local original_version="$1" local target_version="$2" local upgrade_path="$3" # JSON array } # Mark current upgrade as started state_upgrade_start() { local from_version="$1" local to_version="$2" } # Mark current upgrade as completed state_upgrade_complete() { local to_version="$1" } # Mark that system needs reboot state_upgrade_needs_reboot() { # Sets needs_reboot=true, resume_after_reboot=true } # Clear reboot flags after successful resume state_upgrade_resumed() { # Sets needs_reboot=false } # Check if upgrade is complete state_upgrade_is_complete() { # Returns 0 if current version >= target version } # Get current upgrade stage state_upgrade_get_stage() { # Returns: not_started | upgrading | awaiting_reboot | completed } # Record upgrade error state_upgrade_set_error() { local error_msg="$1" } # Clean up upgrade state after completion state_upgrade_cleanup() { # Remove ubuntu_upgrade section entirely } ``` ## Schema Migration Add migration from v2 to v3: - v3 is backwards compatible (new fields are optional) - Existing v2 state files work without modification - ubuntu_upgrade section only added when upgrade starts ## Why Separate State Section? The upgrade state is conceptually different from installation state: - Upgrade happens BEFORE installation - Upgrade may span multiple reboots - Upgrade state should be cleaned up after completion Keeping it in a separate section makes this clear and allows independent cleanup. ## Atomic Write Considerations All state writes already use atomic temp-file+rename pattern. This is critical for upgrade state since we're about to reboot and can't risk corrupted state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:20:15.998308-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","close_reason":"Extended state.sh with 14 upgrade tracking functions (state_upgrade_init, state_upgrade_start, state_upgrade_complete, etc.). Updated schema to v3 with backward compatibility for v2.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:35.258019-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yzm","title":"Enhance SSH disconnection recovery with prominent AlertCard","description":"# Task: Enhance SSH Disconnection Recovery Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu) ## Current State run-installer/page.tsx already has: - GuideCaution: "Do not close the terminal window while installation is running" - GuideTip: "If your internet connection drops, just SSH back in and run again" However, this is buried in the SimplerGuide. A more prominent AlertCard near the install command would be more reassuring. ## Enhancement Add a prominent AlertCard near the install command: ```tsx

Do not panic! If your SSH connection drops during installation:

  1. The installer keeps running on the VPS
  2. Just SSH back in using the same command
  3. Run the installer command again — it will resume where it left off

The installer is designed to be run multiple times safely. If anything fails, you can always re-run it.

``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Prominent AlertCard near the install command (not buried in SimplerGuide) - [ ] Explicitly says "do not panic" - [ ] Explains installer keeps running - [ ] Explains safe to re-run (idempotent) - [ ] Reassuring tone with info variant (blue/calming) ## Note This elevates existing content from SimplerGuide to a more visible location.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:20.981302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","close_reason":"Added prominent AlertCard with Wifi icon near install command explaining connection drop recovery: (1) installer keeps running, (2) SSH back in, (3) re-run safely"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","title":"EPIC: UX Audit Implementation - Round 1 & 2 Recommendations","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:41:41.025777-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:41:41.025777-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","title":"EPIC: UX Audit Implementation - Round 1 & 2 Recommendations","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:41:41.025777-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:12:16.565061-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-31e","title":"Installer: implement (or de-scope) cloud/db installs + skip flags","description":"install.sh defines --skip-postgres/--skip-vault/--skip-cloud and smoke test references them, but install.sh never installs PostgreSQL/Vault/cloud CLIs. scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh + acfs.manifest.yaml define these installs, and README claims they're installed.","acceptance_criteria":"Either: - installer runs cloud/db phase (respecting skip flags) without breaking Ubuntu 24.04/25.x CI, OR - docs/manifest updated to match reality and flags removed/hidden.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:17:29.066623-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cloud/db phase in install.sh (Postgres 18 via PGDG, Vault via HashiCorp repo, cloud CLIs via bun) honoring skip flags; updated smoke test + phase numbering."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","title":"Create 'Starting a New Project' guide section","description":"# Task: Create 'Starting a New Project' Guide Section ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc) ## Description The wizard uses "my-first-project" as an example, but doesn't explain the general pattern for starting real projects. Users need guidance on: - Creating project folders - Initializing git - Setting up with Claude ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx or new /learn/new-project page \`\`\`tsx

Starting a New Project

Ready to build something? Here's the pattern:

1. Create a session for your project

This creates a persistent workspace named "my-awesome-app".

2. Create and navigate to a project folder

3. Start Claude and describe your project

Tell Claude what you want to build. For example:

"Create a React app with TypeScript that shows a todo list"
Claude will set up the project structure, install dependencies, and start building. You can guide it step by step or give it the whole vision at once.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows 3-step pattern (session → folder → Claude) - [ ] Uses concrete example project - [ ] Includes example prompt for Claude - [ ] Explains what Claude will do - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Different color from daily workflow to distinguish - Example project should be relatable (todo app is classic) - Emphasize that Claude does the heavy lifting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:00.569413-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in launch-onboarding with: 3-step pattern (ntm new → mkdir cd → cc), example project 'my-awesome-app', example Claude prompt for React todo app, GuideTip explaining Claude's capabilities, cyan color scheme distinct from daily workflow.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s4cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:33.220094-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","title":"Implement agent auth functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement agent auth functional tests ## Context Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i) ## What to Do Create functional tests for AI agent authentication: ### check_claude_auth() 1. Check if claude binary exists (skip if not) 2. Check if ~/.claude/config.json exists 3. Try claude --print-system-info (low-cost API check) 4. Return: 0=OK, 1=fail, 2=skip ### check_codex_auth() 1. Check if codex binary exists 2. Check for OPENAI_API_KEY env var 3. Check for API key in ~/.zshrc.local 4. Return status ### check_gemini_auth() 1. Check if gemini binary exists 2. Check for GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY 3. Check for credentials file 4. Return status ## Acceptance Criteria - Each check has clear pass/fail criteria - Skipped if tool not installed (status 2) - Auth issues detected reliably - No false positives ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add check functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:18.598224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented check_claude_auth(), check_codex_auth(), check_gemini_auth() with config/API key checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:34.958137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -149,7 +149,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","title":"Implement shell tool updates (omz, p10k, plugins, atuin, zoxide)","description":"## What Implement the 'shell' category for acfs-update: - Oh-My-Zsh: Run $ZSH/tools/upgrade.sh (non-interactive) - Powerlevel10k: git pull --ff-only in $ZSH_CUSTOM/themes/powerlevel10k - zsh-autosuggestions: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - zsh-syntax-highlighting: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - Atuin: Use atuin self-update if available, else reinstall - Zoxide: Reinstall via official installer ## Git-Based Updates (omz, p10k, plugins) - Use git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts - If fast-forward fails: warn and skip (user has local changes) - OMZ has its own upgrade script that handles prompts ## Installer-Based Updates (Atuin, Zoxide) These use curl|sh installers like the stack tools: 1. Atuin: Try `atuin self-update` first (if available) - If no self-update: fetch installer, verify checksum, run 2. Zoxide: Fetch installer, verify checksum against checksums.yaml, run For checksum mismatches: same flow as stack tools (warn, prompt, skip/proceed) ## Considerations - ZSH_CUSTOM defaults to $ZSH/custom if not set - DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true for OMZ non-interactive - Some users may have customized themes/plugins - respect that ## Success Criteria - [ ] All shell tools update without prompts (in --yes mode) - [ ] Git-based updates handle conflicts gracefully - [ ] Atuin/Zoxide checksum verification works - [ ] Before/after versions logged - [ ] Skips missing components","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:10.463809-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell tool updates: omz, p10k, zsh plugins, atuin, zoxide","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.018434-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","title":"[codex.8] Audit web app for incorrect codex auth guidance","description":"## Goal Check apps/web/ for any incorrect guidance about codex-cli authentication. ## Files to Check Based on grep results: - apps/web/lib/jargon.ts - apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx - apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts - apps/web/app/workflow/page.tsx - apps/web/app/page.tsx ## What to Look For 1. Any mention of OPENAI_API_KEY for codex 2. Incorrect auth flow descriptions 3. Wizard steps that suggest API key setup ## Updates Needed 1. Replace API key guidance with OAuth flow description 2. Update any wizard steps about codex setup 3. Ensure jargon/definitions are correct ## Lower Priority Because The web app is less critical than the installer scripts - users who hit the web app will eventually run the installer which (after fixes) will give correct guidance. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex in web app 2. Correct OAuth flow described if mentioned","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:24.840054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","close_reason":"Audited web app - no incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex found. Jargon entry describes Codex without auth details. CAAM references are about credential management (correct).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.94908-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dfc","title":"Fix wizard layout heading causing mobile E2E failures","description":"Playwright mobile projects fail because WizardLayout renders a hidden (md:block)

step title before page content. Tests use page.locator('h1').first(), which resolves to the hidden layout heading on mobile. Also results in multiple h1s per page. Fix by removing or demoting layout heading, ensuring a single visible h1 per wizard page, and update tests if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:03:08.330927-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed on main: apps/web/app/wizard/layout.tsx no longer renders a hidden

step title; only the step indicator remains, so mobile h1 selector mismatch is resolved.","labels":["a11y","ci","e2e","web"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dkhc","title":"Security: restore checksum verification for Atuin installer","description":"install.sh currently installs Atuin via a direct curl of GitHub releases/latest/download/atuin-installer.sh without checksums verification (regression vs acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target). Update checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh to track the new URL (or revert to setup.atuin.sh) and ensure install.sh runs Atuin only via the verified-upstream wrapper; keep Atuin non-fatal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:06:12.681145-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:21.187541-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:21.187541-05:00","close_reason":"Bug already fixed in commit 05d84e3 (2025-12-22 23:05:32). install.sh correctly uses acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target for Atuin with checksum verification. The bead was created 1 minute after the fix was committed."} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dkhc","title":"Security: restore checksum verification for Atuin installer","description":"install.sh currently installs Atuin via a direct curl of GitHub releases/latest/download/atuin-installer.sh without checksums verification (regression vs acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target). Update checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh to track the new URL (or revert to setup.atuin.sh) and ensure install.sh runs Atuin only via the verified-upstream wrapper; keep Atuin non-fatal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:06:12.681145-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:12:07.77406-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:12:07.77406-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed in commit 05d84e3 (Dec 22). Verified: install.sh uses acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_target for Atuin, checksums.yaml has setup.atuin.sh with valid SHA256 (verified against live URL)."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: AlertCard with Laptop icon explaining device auth flow with 5-step process and 'Opening browser...' confusion addressed at lines 179-197"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive unit coverage (no mocks) + E2E logging","description":"# Goal Establish real-data, no-mock unit coverage and complete E2E integration scripts with detailed, human-readable logging. # Why - Current tests are partial (installer E2E + web Playwright). No full unit coverage across core Bash libs or manifest tooling. - E2E logs exist but are not standardized or granular enough for fast diagnosis. # Success Criteria - Unit coverage exists for manifest parser/generator, selection resolver, contract/security/preflight libs with real fixtures (no mocks/fakes). - E2E scripts cover bootstrap + selection + resume flows with structured logs and artifacts. - Tests are deterministic, diffable, and runnable locally + in CI. # Notes - "No mocks/fakes" means: use real manifests, real generated scripts, real flags; avoid stubbing core behaviors. - Logging should be timestamped, sectioned, and include command echo + exit codes. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:13.75871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children completed: coverage matrix, parser/generator tests (132 tests), installer lib tests (15 tests), test harness, selection E2E (26 tests), web E2E runner script.","labels":["qa","tests"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","title":"Define coverage matrix + real fixtures catalog","description":"# Scope Create a concrete coverage matrix that maps each core component to a test type, and standardize a catalog of **real** fixtures used by unit tests. # Deliverables - Coverage matrix table (component → test type → owner → status). - Fixture catalog list (real manifest YAMLs, generated scripts, seed state files) with storage location and usage notes. # Detailed comments - Components to include: `packages/manifest` (schema/parser/generator), `scripts/lib` (install_helpers, contract, security, preflight, logging), `install.sh` selection/introspection, `tests/vm` E2E. - Fixtures must be real (valid YAMLs and generated outputs), not synthetic mocks. - Ensure fixtures are minimal but realistic; avoid brittle snapshots. # Acceptance criteria - Matrix is stored in repo (likely `docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md`). - Fixture catalog references actual files committed in repo. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.951298-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","close_reason":"Added docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md and docs/tests/fixtures_catalog.md with real-file coverage matrix + fixture catalog","labels":["qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.953283-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
